You are working in /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service (NestJS/TypeScript monorepo).

## Your Task
Fix ESLint `custom-rules/require-object-params` violations for Jira ticket RECO-659.
Ticket URL: https://customink.atlassian.net/browse/RECO-659

## Context
- Current branch: `RECO-687-fix-formatting-util-eslint`
- You must create a NEW branch from this branch (NOT from main)
- Branch name format: `RECO-659-<kebab-summary>`
- Commit format: `RECO-659: <imperative description>`

## ESLint Rule
`custom-rules/require-object-params` — all exported functions must use a single object destructuring parameter instead of positional params.

**Pattern:**
```typescript
// ❌ Before
export function myFn(id: string, name: string): void {}
// ✅ After
export function myFn({ id, name }: { id: string; name: string }): void {}
```

## Steps

### 1. Find the target file from the Jira ticket
The ticket title/description will tell you which file to fix. Since you can't access Jira directly, run ESLint across the codebase and focus on the file mentioned in scope. Look at recent similar tickets for clues. The ticket is likely about a specific file in `src/`.

Try running:
```bash
npx eslint --max-warnings=0 src/ --format=compact 2>&1 | grep "require-object-params" | head -30
```
to find files with violations. Cross-reference with ticket numbering pattern (RECO-659 is between RECO-660, RECO-686, etc. which are also ESLint fix tickets).

Actually - look at the Jira ticket URL pattern. These tickets (RECO-659, 660, 686-694, 696) are all ESLint fix tickets for specific files. Run ESLint and pick the file that makes sense for this ticket number range.

### 2. Create branch
```bash
git checkout -b RECO-659-<descriptive-name>
```
Branch from current branch `RECO-687-fix-formatting-util-eslint`.

### 3. Run ESLint on target file
```bash
npx eslint --max-warnings=0 <target-file> 2>&1
```

### 4. Fix violations
- Change positional params to `{ param }: { param: type }` destructuring
- Update ALL internal calls to the changed functions
- Find ALL callers using grep: `grep -r "functionName" src/ test/ --include="*.ts" -l`
- Update every call site
- Do NOT use `{ ...param }` spread shortcuts

### 5. Update tests
Find and update all test files that call the changed functions.

### 6. Run tests
```bash
npm run test:unit -- --testPathPatterns="<test-file-pattern>" 2>&1
```
All tests must pass.

### 7. Run type-check
```bash
npm run type-check 2>&1
```
Must be clean.

### 8. Verify ESLint on changed files
```bash
npx eslint --max-warnings=0 <changed-files> 2>&1
```

### 9. Commit (only relevant files — NOT .husky/pre-push or unrelated files)
```bash
git add <specific-files-only>
git commit -m "RECO-659: <description>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
```

### 10. Push
```bash
git push -u origin RECO-659-<branch-name>
```

### 11. Create DRAFT PR
Base branch = `RECO-687-fix-formatting-util-eslint` (NOT main).

Use this PR template:
```
### Ticket 🐗

JIRA task: [RECO-659](https://customink.atlassian.net/browse/RECO-659)
Design: N/A

### Changes 🍍

<describe changes>

### PR Checks 🐊

- [x] I've self-reviewed my code.
- [x] I've added the necessary labels.
- [x] I've added tests for my code.
- [ ] I've updated the documentation.

### Description / Ticket Summary 🐙

<ticket summary>

### Screenshots / Short Videos 🐮

N/A
```

```bash
gh pr create --draft --base RECO-687-fix-formatting-util-eslint --title "RECO-659: ..." --body "..."
```

## Critical Rules
- NEVER modify `.husky/pre-push`
- NEVER use relative imports (use `@utils/`, `@modules/`, etc.)
- NO comments in code
- All functions need explicit return types
- Do NOT use `{ ...spread }` as shortcut for updating callers

## Return
Return:
1. The new branch name created
2. The PR URL
3. Summary of files changed and what was fixed
